vocabulary.meaning: to have an influence on or make a difference to
vocabulary.example: The cold weather can affect your health if you don't dress warmly.

vocabulary.meaning: to keep in a steady position, or a state where things are equal or in correct proportions
vocabulary.example: Try to balance work and leisure time. / We need to maintain a healthy balance in our diet.

vocabulary.meaning: to receive an advantage, or an advantage or profit gained from something
vocabulary.example: Regular exercise will benefit your cardiovascular system. / There are many health benefits to drinking water.

vocabulary.meaning: to take air into the lungs and send it out again
vocabulary.example: Take a deep breath and breathe out slowly.

vocabulary.meaning: to crush food with your teeth before swallowing it
vocabulary.example: You should chew your food thoroughly before swallowing.

vocabulary.meaning: to cut something into pieces with repeated sharp blows
vocabulary.example: Chop the onions and carrots into small pieces for the soup.

vocabulary.meaning: to have or hold something within
vocabulary.example: Oranges contain a large amount of Vitamin C.

vocabulary.meaning: to force air out of the lungs with a sudden sharp sound, or an illness that causes coughing
vocabulary.example: He coughed to clear his throat. / She has a bad cough and a sore throat.

vocabulary.meaning: to make someone healthy again, or a medicine or treatment that cures an illness
vocabulary.example: Doctors managed to cure the patient's illness. / There is currently no cure for the common cold.

vocabulary.meaning: to do physical activity, or physical activity that you do to make your body strong
vocabulary.example: She exercises in the gym three times a week. / Daily exercise is important for physical fitness.

vocabulary.meaning: an infectious disease that causes fever, chills, and muscle aches
vocabulary.example: He missed school because he caught the flu.

vocabulary.meaning: to undergo a medical procedure where a doctor cuts into the body to repair or remove a part
vocabulary.example: She had to have an operation on her knee after the sports injury.

vocabulary.meaning: in a good physical or mental condition; not ill
vocabulary.example: Eating fresh vegetables is part of a healthy lifestyle.

vocabulary.meaning: to refuse to take notice of or pay attention to someone or something
vocabulary.example: You shouldn't ignore the warning signs of stress.

vocabulary.meaning: a disease caused by germs or bacteria entering the body
vocabulary.example: Wash the cut to prevent any bacterial infection.

vocabulary.meaning: one of the foods that you use to make a particular dish
vocabulary.example: Mix all the ingredients together in a large bowl.

vocabulary.meaning: physical damage or harm done to a person's body
vocabulary.example: He recovered quickly from his minor leg injury.

vocabulary.meaning: to keep something within a certain size or amount, or the greatest amount allowed
vocabulary.example: You should limit the amount of sugar you consume daily. / There is a speed limit of fifty miles per hour.

vocabulary.meaning: an occasion when food is eaten, or the food eaten on such an occasion
vocabulary.example: Breakfast is considered the most important meal of the day.

vocabulary.meaning: a small round piece of medicine that you swallow
vocabulary.example: The doctor told him to take one pill after each meal.

vocabulary.meaning: to become well again after being ill or injured
vocabulary.example: It took her several weeks to recover from the surgery.

vocabulary.meaning: tasting of or containing salt
vocabulary.example: I don't like eating salty snacks.

vocabulary.meaning: to cut food into thin, flat pieces, or a thin, flat piece of food
vocabulary.example: Can you slice the bread for the sandwiches? / He ate a slice of chocolate cake.

vocabulary.meaning: having an acid taste like lemon or vinegar
vocabulary.example: These green grapes are very sour.

vocabulary.meaning: containing strong spices or having a hot flavor
vocabulary.example: This spicy soup made my eyes water.

vocabulary.meaning: to mix liquids or food by moving a spoon around in a circular pattern
vocabulary.example: Stir the soup occasionally while it is boiling.

vocabulary.meaning: to experience physical or mental pain, or an illness
vocabulary.example: Many people suffer from seasonal allergies in the spring.

vocabulary.meaning: to have a particular flavor, or the flavor of something
vocabulary.example: The soup tastes delicious. / This fruit has a very sweet taste.

vocabulary.meaning: the use of drugs or exercises to cure an illness or injury
vocabulary.example: She is receiving medical treatment for her back pain.

vocabulary.meaning: a natural substance found in food that is necessary for good health
vocabulary.example: Fresh fruits are rich in vitamins and minerals.

vocabulary.meaning: a widespread occurrence of an infectious disease in a community at a particular time
vocabulary.example: The city took strict measures to prevent a flu epidemic.

vocabulary.meaning: a serious disease passed to humans by mosquitoes, causing fever and chills
vocabulary.example: You should take malaria tablets if you travel to tropical regions.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to reduce the amount or consumption of something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The doctor advised him to cut down on sugar.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to slip or trip and fall to the ground
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He tripped over the rug and fell down on the floor.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to recover from an illness, shock, or difficult situation
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: It took her a long time to get over the flu.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to become bad or stale (food or drink)
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Put the milk in the fridge so it doesn't go off.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to place oneself in a flat, horizontal position (usually to rest)
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I'm feeling a bit dizzy, so I'm going to lie down for a while.
EX: He put on a few kilograms over the holidays.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to lower one's body into a sitting position on a chair or the ground
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Please sit down and make yourself comfortable.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to rise to an erect, standing position
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Everyone stood up when the teacher entered the room.

prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The streets are very quiet at night.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: in danger of being harmed or lost
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: People who don't exercise are at risk of heart disease.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: as an extra thing or person
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: In addition to French, she speaks fluent Spanish.
EX: The new model is much faster in comparison with the old one.
EX: He runs every morning to keep in shape.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: eating less or specific food to lose weight or improve health
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: She is on a diet, so she avoids sugary desserts.

wordFormation.meaning: baker: a person who bakes bread and cakes / bakery: a shop where bread and cakes are made and sold
wordFormation.example: EX: The baker prepared fresh loaves of bread. / I bought these pastries at the local bakery.

wordFormation.meaning: bent: curved or not straight; or a natural talent or inclination
wordFormation.example: EX: The metal rod was bent. / She has a natural bent for painting.

wordFormation.meaning: cooking: the activity of preparing food / cooker: an appliance used for cooking / cookery: the art or practice of cooking
wordFormation.example: EX: She enjoys cooking Italian dishes. / We bought a new electric cooker for the kitchen. / He is taking a cookery course this semester.

wordFormation.meaning: intention: an aim, plan, or purpose / intentional: done on purpose; deliberate
wordFormation.example: EX: He has no intention of leaving his job. / The referee ruled that the foul was intentional.

wordFormation.meaning: jogging: the activity of running at a steady, gentle pace / jogger: a person who jogs
wordFormation.example: EX: Jogging is a great way to stay fit. / The park was filled with joggers in the morning.

wordFormation.meaning: medical: relating to the practice of medicine or treatment
wordFormation.example: EX: She is undergoing medical treatment for her knee injury.

wordFormation.meaning: painful: causing physical pain / painless: causing no physical pain
wordFormation.example: EX: His broken arm was very painful. / The dentist promised that the treatment would be painless.

wordFormation.meaning: reduction: the action of making something smaller or less in amount
wordFormation.example: EX: There was a significant reduction in prices during the sale.

wordFormation.meaning: sensible: having or showing good sense or judgment / sensitive: easily affected or offended
wordFormation.example: EX: It is sensible to wear a seatbelt while driving. / His eyes are very sensitive to bright light.

wordFormation.meaning: weight: how heavy someone or something is
wordFormation.example: EX: He is trying to lose some weight by exercising.

wordPatterns.meaning: physically or mentally dependent on a substance or activity
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is addicted to playing video games.

wordPatterns.meaning: having a strong reaction or illness caused by pollen, food, or other substances
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is allergic to peanuts.

wordPatterns.meaning: having a layer of something on the surface
wordPatterns.example: EX: The ground was covered in white snow.
EX: The teacher was very pleased with our test results.

wordPatterns.meaning: to join two or more things together
wordPatterns.example: EX: You can combine study with part-time work.

wordPatterns.meaning: to express dissatisfaction or annoyance about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: The residents complained to the council about the noise.

wordPatterns.meaning: to stop living as a result of an illness or cause
wordPatterns.example: EX: Many people die of heart disease every year.

wordPatterns.meaning: to struggle or oppose someone/something in combat or competition
wordPatterns.example: EX: The local community is fighting against the new highway plan.

wordPatterns.meaning: to become healthy again after an illness
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is still recovering from a bad cold.

wordPatterns.meaning: to have the scent of a particular thing
wordPatterns.example: EX: The kitchen smells of freshly baked bread.

wordPatterns.meaning: a medicine or treatment that cures a disease
wordPatterns.example: EX: Scientists are trying to find a cure for cancer.

wordPatterns.meaning: a set of instructions for preparing a particular dish
wordPatterns.example: EX: She gave me a great recipe for chocolate cookies.
